Empires & Puzzles Zarola Review: The 3-Star Cupid of Heartbreakers
Zarola brings the pain to enemies who haven't charged their specials yet. Discover why this Season of Love ranger is a staple for Challenge Events and 3-star tournaments in our comprehensive guide.
Zarola: The 3-Star Sniper Who Loves an Empty Mana Bar
Zarola, The Heartbreaking Huntress: Punishing Low Mana with High Speed
Zarola is a 3-star Nature (Green) hero from the

event, originally released in February 2023. Designed as a fast-paced damage dealer, she fits the archetype of a "punisher" hero—someone who capitalizes on the enemy's lack of readiness. As a Ranger, she has the potential to bypass defensive buffs, making her a nasty surprise in 3-star tournaments. While she hails from a few years back, her unique mechanics keep her relevant in specific niches. She doesn't have a costume, so this review focuses entirely on her base form. And yes, she looks like she's about to shoot you with a heart-shaped arrow, but don't let the cuteness fool you; that crossbow is loaded with malice.

Zarola sports a classic "Robin Hood meets Valentine's Day" aesthetic. Clad in forest greens with heart-shaped emerald accents, she wields a crossbow that looks surprisingly heavy for a cherub-adjacent character. Her expression is a mix of mischievous glee and focused determination, suggesting she enjoys the hunt a little too much. The art style fits well with the whimsical yet dangerous vibe of the Cupid family, balancing the saccharine theme of love with the violence of a combat game.
When looking at Zarola's stats in the context of the current 2026 meta, she shows her age. With a base power of 486 and attack of 547, she falls into the

category compared to modern Toon costumes or the latest event 3-stars which push stats significantly higher. However, her attack stat is still respectable for a 3-star damage dealer, especially when heavily emblemed and limit broken. She isn't a glass cannon, but she certainly isn't a tank either; she sits comfortably in the middle, relying on her speed rather than raw bulk to survive.

200% damage to the target and nearby enemies.
The less mana the enemies have, the more damage they receive, scaling up to 300%.
This mechanic makes her an absolute monster in

and the opening turns of a Raid. In PvE events, bosses start with zero mana, guaranteeing that Zarola hits for her maximum potential right out of the gate. At Fast speed, she can often fire before the enemy has gathered significant mana, maintaining high damage output. However, in prolonged battles where enemies are constantly charging, her damage floor of 200% can feel a bit whelming compared to newer heavy hitters.

The resistance to Special Skill Blocking is situational. In the 3-star arena, you don't see as many silencers (like Miki or Peters), but it protects her from the likes of Pixie or Treevil's mana slow (though not the silence itself if it's a different mechanic).
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

provides increased healing (+20%) for "Solo" heroes. A hero is considered Solo if they have no other family or realm members in the team. This is a bit counter-intuitive for Zarola herself unless you run her as the

Cupid in a rainbow team or a mixed family team. If you stack multiple Zarolas (a popular strategy), you lose this bonus. However, since Zarola is pure damage and has no self-heal, this bonus relies entirely on you bringing a healer from a

This makes her immune to ailments that specifically prevent special skills from firing (like Silence or Spell Slay). While less common in the 3-star rarity compared to Legendary tiers, it is a nice insurance policy against heroes like Pixie or when punching up in War against 4-star silencers like Peters.

Upon Limit Breaking, her special skill damage is increased. This is a simple, effective boost that plays directly to her strength as a damage dealer. It helps bridge the gap created by power creep, ensuring her "Punishing Shot" still stings.

Upon her release, Zarola was the subject of heated debate due to a significant nerf from Beta (where she reportedly dealt up to 360% damage). Despite the nerf, she quickly became a staple for competitive Challenge Event players who realized her mechanic was perfect for boss waves.
In 2026, raw percentage damage is common. We have 3-star heroes hitting for 300%+ regularly. However, a
Hit-3 that can hit for 300% is still premium. Her stats are the main victim of power creep; she lacks the massive HP and Defense of the "Toon" meta.
in specific niches. She is not a "set and forget" defense hero anymore, but for
, she is still a top-tier offensive pick. She has transitioned from a generalist to a specialized weapon.
Current status: Zarola does not have a costume.

This review focuses on the base version. If a costume were to be released, one would hope for a stat boost to bring her inline with 2026 standards and perhaps a secondary effect on her special skill, as pure damage is easily outscaled.
As a Ranger, Zarola has a chance to bypass defensive buffs (Pierce). This is excellent for a damage dealer.

If you pull Zarola, level her immediately. She will be your best friend for completing the Rare tier of monthly Challenge Events, which is the best source of ascension materials for new players. She is cheap to level and effective without complex setups.
You likely have her maxed already. If not, she is worth a roster spot solely for the

events restricted to 3-stars. Is she worth a second Limit Break (Alpha Aether)? Probably not, unless you are chasing top 100 in Rare events. Save the Alpha Aethers for heroes with more universal utility or superior stats.
When Zarola was released, the community was up in arms about her "stealth nerf" from Beta.
"I was going to keep 3, but now it’s sadly 0."

"Put 3 of her with Brienne and Dolgoon for Rare Events, and they’ll tear the bosses with their 0 mana apart."
History has proven the latter group correct. While the nerf stung, a Fast 300% Hit-3 hero was never going to be "bad." She remains a favorite for efficient farming and event placement.

Zarola is a specialized tool in an increasingly complex toolbox. She isn't the "Cupid" who brings people together; she's the one who tears them apart before they've even had their morning coffee (or mana potion). While her stats are showing some wrinkles, her "Punishing Shot" mechanic ensures she remains a top pick for content where speed and burst damage are paramount.
A mandatory keep for Event enthusiasts, and a solid sniper for everyone else.
A detailed review of Zarola, the 3-star Nature Ranger from the Season of Love. We analyze her stats, Punishing Shot skill mechanics, and viability in tournaments and events.
